steps.prompt: Which word is the finite verb?

hints.timere: 'timere' is an infinitive, not a finite verb
hints.nolle: 'nolle' is the infinitive of 'nolo' — 'Noli' is the imperative form
hints.time: 'time' does not appear in this phrase — 'Noli' is the finite verb

steps.target: to be unwilling / to not want
choices: to be unwilling / to not want

hints.to know: 'to know' is 'nosse/scire' — 'Noli' comes from 'nolle' (to be unwilling)
hints.to harm: 'to harm' is 'nocere' — 'Noli' comes from 'nolle' (to be unwilling)
hints.to deny: 'to deny' is 'negare' — 'Noli' comes from 'nolle' (to be unwilling)

hints.present indicative: Present indicative 1st person is 'nolo' — 'Noli' is the imperative singular
hints.subjunctive: Subjunctive would be 'nolim' — 'Noli' is the imperative used for prohibitions
hints.perfect: Perfect would be 'nolui' — 'Noli' is the imperative

hints.nosco, noscere, novi, notum: These are principal parts of 'nosco' (to get to know)
hints.noceo, nocere, nocui, nocitum: These are principal parts of 'noceo' (to harm)
hints.nego, negare, negavi, negatum: These are principal parts of 'nego' (to deny)

hints.imperative: Imperative would be 'time' — 'timere' ending in -ere is the infinitive
hints.participle: Participle would be 'timens' or 'timitus' — 'timere' is the infinitive
hints.gerund: Gerund would be 'timendi' — 'timere' is the present active infinitive

hints.to tremble: 'to tremble' is 'tremere' — 'timere' means 'to fear' (related but distinct)

hints.teneo, tenere, tenui, tentum: These are principal parts of 'teneo' (to hold)
hints.taceo, tacere, tacui, tacitum: These are principal parts of 'taceo' (to be silent)
hints.tremo, tremere, tremui, —: These are principal parts of 'tremo' (to tremble)
